title: Why MCP and ChatGPT Apps Use Double Iframes — Frédéric Barthelet, Alpic
summary: Double iframe pattern isolates third-party apps in ChatGPT and MCP by nesting an srcdoc iframe within a sandboxed iframe loaded from a controlled subdomain. This avoids CSP violations, prevents localStorage/cookie access, blocks sandbox escapes, and allows per-app subdomain isolation. All external domains must be declared in MCP metadata.
writeup: This talk explains the double iframe pattern that ChatGPT and MCP use to isolate third-party apps: an srcdoc iframe nested inside a sandboxed iframe loaded from a controlled subdomain. The nesting matters because it avoids CSP violations, blocks access to localStorage and cookies, prevents sandbox escapes, and enables per-app isolation by subdomain. The session also notes that all external domains must be declared in MCP metadata.
